item.title: Getting Creative With Images in Long-Form Content
item.author: Andy Clarke

item.title: Learning web development: Shells and Node.js
item.author: Dr. Axel Rauschmayer
item.content:encoded: In this chapter we explore two topics:A is like browser console, but for the operating system instead of for JavaScript. It helps us with programming by running the tools (programs) we need to get things done. is a program that lets us run JavaScript code outside browsers – which we can use for a variety of things.

item.title: Learning web development: Loops in JavaScript
item.author: Dr. Axel Rauschmayer
item.content:encoded: In this chapter, we learn how to do things repeatedly in JavaScript.

item.title: 3D Layered Text: Interactivity and Dynamicism
item.author: Amit Sheen

item.title: We still love jQuery
item.content:encoded: — Lee, formerly of Vercel and widely recognized for his influence on Next.js and React, shares candid reflections on the React community, digging into the rise of React Server Components, the tension between commercial and non-commercial priorities, the toll of burnout, and a reminder that, above all, this is still a community of

item.title: 3D Layered Text: Motion and Variations
item.author: Amit Sheen

item.title: Learning web development: Booleans, comparisons and if statements
item.author: Dr. Axel Rauschmayer
item.content:encoded: In this chapter, we learn about tools for only running a piece of code if a condition is met: truth values (booleans), comparisons and statements.

item.title: This Week In React #246: Next.js, React Router, TanStack, StyleX, MUI, Storybook | FlashList 2, RN 0.81, Expo, Radon, Gesture Handler, Audio, Skia, Nitro, Strict DOM | TC39, Node, TypeScript, Astro, BIDC, Surveys
item.content:encoded: After a short summer break, we’re happy to say we’re back! I think we could open a public swimming pool because we’re drowning in the amount of content this week.This week was full of big releases: Vercel released Next.js 15.5, React Native 0.81 was launched, and last but not least, we finally have the long-awaited FlashList 2 with great performance and cool features.As always, thanks for supporting us on your favorite platform:Your data grid should do more than just render rows. Performance matters, but UX is where the real wins happen. When picking a React Data Grid, consider how users interact with it and what features will help them get things done. Ignite UI for React goes beyond the basics. It provides everything you need to build high-performance web apps with must-haves like:🔍 Advanced Filtering – Complex queries with AND/OR logic, no code required.💾 State Persistence – Save grid layouts, filters & sort state across sessions.⌨️ Keyboard Navigation – Faster workflows + built-in accessibility.📤 Export to Excel – Share filtered data in a click.📊 Pivot Grid – Analyze data without leaving your app.See how you can utilize these features and maximize the potential of your next React app.The new minor version of Next.js brings exciting improvements across many areas of the framework including: in middleware now marked as stableTurbopack for production builds in betaTyped Routes for type-safe navigationDeprecation of in order to provide Biome as a fast alternative to ESLintNext.js 16 deprecation warningsDiscover a new perspective on building full-stack web applications with and . A hands-on course that teaches you how to build real-world SaaS applications, including payment gateways, message queues, database inspectors, and email servers.Learn a cutting-edge stack with and from bestselling book author and blogger .FlashList v2 is a complete rewrite that eliminates the need for developers to provide item size estimates, a major limitation of v1. Thanks to React Native's New Architecture, which enables synchronous layout measurements, FlashList v2 can precisely calculate and correct item positions before they are painted on the screen. This new approach removes the need for native code and delivers pixel-perfect scrolling, improves performance with an adaptive rendering algorithm, and enables advanced features like automatic scroll position maintenance. Also, FlashList v2 comes with masonry layout support built-in.React Native version 0.81 introduces support for the new Android 16, which enforces display requirements. A major new experimental feature is precompiled builds for iOS, promising significantly faster compilation times. Since this version the built-in component is deprecated and they moved JSC runtime support out to a community-maintained package.

item.title: Launching MDN's new front end
item.author: mdn-team
item.content:encoded: MDN is getting a facelift 🎉. Discover what's changed, what's improved, and how navigating the site just got smoother.
